metadata.title: UK Infrastructure Delivery Delays
metadata.description: Major UK government projects tracked from approval through delivery, measuring schedule slippage and cost growth. All timelines sourced from NAO, IPA, and official project updates.
metadata.methodology: Delay calculated as difference between original planned completion and latest estimated/actual completion. Cost growth measured from original budget at main investment decision to latest published estimate. Only projects with verifiable official schedule data included.
sources: Infrastructure and Projects Authority (IPA) Annual Reports

projects.delayCauseDetail: Northern leg and Birmingham-Crewe leg cancelled. Remaining Phase 1 subject to repeated schedule revisions. COVID, HS2 Ltd management changes, ground conditions.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2026 date from 2012 command paper. Latest date reflects Phase 1 only (London-Birmingham). Northern legs cancelled October 2023.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Signalling and systems integration failures. Inadequate project governance. Multiple senior leadership changes. Bond Street station delayed further.
projects.methodologyNote: Original Dec 2018 opening date. Full through-running achieved May 2022. Bond Street station opened later.

projects.delayCauseDetail: First-of-a-kind EPR reactor design. Ground conditions, COVID disruption, concrete supply issues. EDF revised schedule in January 2024.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2025 date from FID (2016). Latest 2031 from EDF January 2024 update. Cost figures in 2015 prices; outturn cost higher.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Excessive noise and vibration caused hearing damage to soldiers during trials. Safety concerns halted testing. Fundamental design remediation required.
projects.methodologyNote: Original IOC 2017 per contract award. Latest IOC November 2025 per MoD update. Vehicle deliveries ongoing but capability limited.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Transition from Airwave to 4G/5G-based network repeatedly delayed. Technology not proven at required coverage levels. Home Office reset programme in 2023.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2019 completion from 2015 business case. Programme reset in 2023 with new delivery approach. Latest cost includes extended Airwave contract.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Loss of UK nuclear submarine design skills after long gap between classes. BAE Systems required extensive US support. Each boat delayed sequentially.
projects.methodologyNote: Original IOC 2005 per 1997 contract. Boat 1 (Astute) commissioned 2010. Boat 7 (Agincourt) expected mid-2020s. Programme costs at 2023 prices.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Government deliberately delayed production in 2008 SDSR to spread costs. Design changed from STOVL to CATOBAR then back to STOVL. Second carrier nearly cancelled.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2012 ISD per 1998 SDR. HMS Queen Elizabeth commissioned 2017, HMS Prince of Wales 2019. Cost figure includes both ships.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Paused in January 2022 following PAC report on safety. New ALR smart motorways cancelled. Existing schemes to have refuge areas retrofitted. Retrofit programme ongoing.
projects.methodologyNote: Programme paused January 2022. No new ALR schemes. Retrofit programme for existing schemes continuing. Cost figures partial.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Original agile IT approach failed. Programme reset in 2013 after write-off of early IT spend. Migration from legacy benefits took far longer than planned.
projects.methodologyNote: Original April 2017 full rollout per 2010 business case. Managed migration from legacy benefits ongoing. Cost is IT programme only, not benefit payments.

projects.delayCauseDetail: COVID-19 lockdowns disrupted tunnelling. Unexpected ground conditions at some shaft sites. Commissioning extended.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2023 completion. Tunnelling completed 2024. Full commissioning expected 2025. Cost at 2014 prices.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Largest civilian IT project ever attempted. Multiple suppliers failed to deliver. Accenture exited. Programme formally dismantled September 2013 with most objectives unmet.
projects.methodologyNote: Programme started 2002, original 2010 target. Formally dismantled 2013. Total spend £9.8bn against £6.2bn approved budget.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Multiple delivery models proposed. Sponsor Body dissolved March 2024. New programme authority established. Full decant vs rolling programme still debated. No confirmed start date.
projects.methodologyNote: Cost range £7-22bn depending on delivery model. No confirmed timeline. Sponsor Body dissolved. Delay cannot be calculated as no firm completion date has ever been set.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Rolls-Royce reactor manufacturing delays. BAE Systems Barrow yard capacity constraints shared with Astute programme. IPA rated programme Amber/Red.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2028 in-service date per 2016 Main Gate. Schedule pressure acknowledged in IPA 2023-24 report. Boat 1 (Dreadnought) steel cut 2016. Cost at outturn prices.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Dependent on EU EES (Entry/Exit System) which was itself delayed multiple times. UK ETA phased rollout began 2024.
projects.methodologyNote: Delay partly attributable to EU EES system delays (original 2022, now October 2025). UK ETA phased launch began for Gulf nationals 2023, expanded 2024.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Repeatedly descoped in Network North (October 2023). Full new line between Liverpool and Manchester replaced with upgrades. No confirmed delivery date.
projects.sourceName: DfT Network North / Transport for the North
projects.methodologyNote: Original scope from 2019 strategic outline case. October 2023 Network North significantly reduced ambition. Delay cannot be calculated as project fundamentally changed.

projects.delayCauseDetail: NAO found original programme was unrealistic and not adequately prioritised. Planning permission delays at multiple sites. Only 6,518 of 20,000 target places delivered by September 2024.
projects.methodologyNote: Original 2025 target from 2020 spending review. Revised to 2031 per 2024 NAO report. Cost includes both new-build prisons and expansion of existing sites.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Major IT modernisation programme with Atos. NAO rated programme Red. Significant cost escalation and timeline extension. Programme reset in 2023.
projects.methodologyNote: Original March 2024 completion from 2020 business case. Revised to March 2028 per NAO 2025 report.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Original 2020 manifesto pledge of 40 new hospitals by 2030. Revised to 32 by 2030 with 8 deferred. RAAC concrete crisis reprioritised some schemes. NAO found some hospitals will not begin construction until 2039.
projects.methodologyNote: Delay measured from original 2030 target to latest NAO-reported start dates for deferred schemes. Total programme cost not confirmed. Individual scheme costs vary.

projects.delayCauseDetail: Post-EU exit customs systems replacement. Original scope expanded. Budget more than doubled from original approval. NAO found delays hampered border ambitions.
projects.methodologyNote: Original March 2025 from April 2022 business case. Revised to March 2027 per NAO report. Budget progression: £243m (2022) → £309m (2024) → £570m (2025).

projects.delayCauseDetail: 21-mile road upgrade. Opened May 2020, two months late due to adverse weather. Delivered broadly on budget. One of few major road schemes to complete near schedule.
projects.methodologyNote: Opened May 2020 vs original March 2020 target. Minor delay. Included as positive comparator.

projects.delayCauseDetail: 11-year modernisation programme. Additional £124m secured in 2025. By March 2024, 68% of original budget spent with only 18% of timeline completed. Expected returns cut from £680m to £531m.
projects.methodologyNote: No schedule delay confirmed yet, but significant budget pressure. Included for cost growth tracking. Spend rate suggests possible future delay.

projects.delayCauseDetail: DCO approved August 2023 after judicial review. Cost estimate rose from £1.9bn to £2.35bn (+47%). Cancelled by government July 2024 due to cost concerns. £461m already spent written off.
projects.methodologyNote: Cancelled after DCO approval. Planning delay (tracked separately) led to cost escalation during approval period. Sunk cost of £461m.

delayTimelineMethodology: Average schedule slippage and cost growth across IPA-tracked major projects, sampled biennially. Based on IPA Annual Reports and NAO Major Projects reports. Projects enter and exit the sample as they complete or are cancelled.
